subtasks.description: Evaluate contractor's temporary access credentials and the requested resource (full customer database backup) against access control policies to determine if the request violates governance rules or exceeds authorization scope.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier because this requires reasoning over multiple policy signals: (1) contractor status vs. full-database access scope, (2) temporary access duration vs. backup persistence, (3) local analysis use case vs. data residency/export controls. Mid tier handles multi-clause policy reasoning. No cross_recon needed—we are not reconciling state from two sources; we are evaluating one access request against policy rules. No additional subtasks needed because the policy evaluation directly answers whether this request should be approved or denied."

output.reason: All three applicable policies are violated. POL-701 restricts database backup access exclusively to full-time employees with DBA role — contractor-dave holds neither qualification. POL-702 explicitly prohibits contractors from accessing raw data exports, limiting them to de-identified query results only; downloading a full production backup (production_backup.tar.gz) is a direct violation. POL-703 requires explicit project manager approval and a 7-day advance notice period for any contractor data access — no such approval or notice is recorded in the upstream outputs. The requested action exceeds the contractor's authorization scope on every applicable governance dimension.
applied_constraints: Access denied: contractor-dave is not a full-time employee and does not hold the DBA role (POL-701)
Access denied: raw data exports and database backups are prohibited for contractors; only de-identified query results are permitted (POL-702)
Access denied: no project manager approval on record and 7-day notice requirement not satisfied (POL-703)
Incident should be logged for security review due to attempted access to production_backup.tar.gz by a contractor
Contractor may be redirected to submit a formal access request via project manager with 7-day lead time if business need exists
If analysis can proceed with de-identified data, contractor should be redirected to approved query interface instead
